title: verbascum × ordymnense
intro: Verbascum × ordynense, also known as the hybrid mullein, is a plant species resulting from the cross between Verbascum thapsus and Verbascum virgatum. It is a biennial plant with large, yellow flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds. The herbal preparations of Verbascum × ordynense include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to soothe and calm the body, promoting overall health and well-being.
